title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: The yen sits at a four-decade low and Tokyo has changed tactics. Officials signalled an ambush posture, using surprise operations against crowded short positions, while the official ledger still shows zero disclosed intervention through 26 June. On 2 July the yen bounced toward 160.9 on a weak US payrolls print, though the US-Japan rate gap keeps the pressure on.

Brent slid to a four-month low near $71 as the Gulf war premium drained. Qatari and Pakistani mediators reported progress in Doha and Hormuz kept flowing, so buyers priced open lanes over disruption. A single fresh incident would put the premium back overnight.

Seoul and Washington are steering billions toward power-rich land and domestic chips while China ships cheap open-weight models. Semiconductor stocks fell on 2 July, with the SOXX index down 4.4%, as investors asked whether AI-spend optimism was already in the price.

props.caption: The US and Korea bind compute to power and land; China makes the model cheap to download

items.why: The finance ministry has swapped telegraphed level-defence for surprise; a confirmed operation, or a hawkish Bank of Japan turn, would tell markets whether the threat has teeth or the rate gap keeps winning.
The desk's open call sits at p=0.38 against a sub-158 print; watch the MOF disclosure index and US yields for the first real sign the trend has turned.
Washington declined to renew the pact in its current form, starting a rolling-review clock; the next meeting sets whether revision or drift follows.
Korea and the US are binding models to power and land while China ships cheap open weights; watch whether territorial capex or downloadable efficiency sets the deployment terms.
Nearly 500 drones in one night reopened the debate over interceptor stocks; watch whether European capitals accelerate mobile batteries and ammunition reloads.

open_calls.question: USD/JPY trades below 158.00
open_calls.detail: The US-Japan rate gap and record-crowded shorts favour a weak yen, and the finance-ministry ledger still shows zero disclosed intervention through 26 June; one payroll-driven bounce toward 160.9 has not reversed the trend from its four-decade extreme near 162.7.

items.what: Spot gold rose about 2.4% to roughly $4,127 an ounce after a US payroll miss weakened the dollar and eased near-term Fed-tightening fears.
US Strategic Petroleum Reserve stocks fell to 325.7 million barrels, the lowest since 1983, even as spot crude softened on faded Gulf war risk.
US datacenter electricity demand is projected to climb from 31 gigawatts in 2025 to 66 by 2027, pushing datacenter investors to buy up power developers.
